title: fumaria officinalis subsp. ragusina
intro: Fumaria officinalis subsp. ragusina, also known as the Lesser Fumitory, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the fumitory family, characterized by its small, white or yellowish flowers and narrow, lance-shaped leaves. Traditionally, the Lesser Fumitory has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and flavonoids. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the Lesser Fumitory is typically used as a dried herb or in the form of tinctures, teas, or infusions, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
